verses.text: The LORD answers Moses: now you shall see what I will do to Pharaoh. With a strong hand he shall let them go; with a strong hand he shall drive them out. Guidance: God's first response to Moses' complaint is not explanation but promise. He doesn't always defend His delay — He shows you the door He is about to open.

God spoke to Moses: I am the LORD. I appeared to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob as God Almighty, but by My name LORD I was not fully known to them. Guidance: A new generation is given a new depth of God's name. What your ancestors believed in part, you may come to know more completely.

God spoke to Moses: I am the LORD. I appeared to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob as God Almighty, but by My name LORD I was not fully known to them. Guidance: A new generation is given a new depth of God's name. What your ancestors believed in part, you may come to know more completely.

I established My covenant with them — to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their pilgrimage. I have also heard the groaning of the children of Israel whom the Egyptians enslave; I have remembered My covenant. Guidance: God's memory is His action. When Scripture says God remembered, it means He is about to act.

I established My covenant with them — to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their pilgrimage. I have also heard the groaning of the children of Israel whom the Egyptians enslave; I have remembered My covenant. Guidance: God's memory is His action. When Scripture says God remembered, it means He is about to act.

Therefore tell them: I am the LORD. I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians. I will rid you of their bondage. I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and great judgments. Guidance: Three I-will promises in a single verse — bring out, rid, redeem. Slow down and let each one land.

I will take you to Me for a people, and I will be to you a God. You shall know I am the LORD. I will bring you into the land I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. I will give it to you for an inheritance: I am the LORD. Guidance: Deliverance is not only from something — it is to Someone. Freedom is meant to land you in a relationship, not just a new address.

I will take you to Me for a people, and I will be to you a God. You shall know I am the LORD. I will bring you into the land I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. I will give it to you for an inheritance: I am the LORD. Guidance: Deliverance is not only from something — it is to Someone. Freedom is meant to land you in a relationship, not just a new address.

Moses tells the people, but they do not listen — because of their anguish of spirit and cruel bondage. Guidance: Sometimes hope cannot get in past the day's pain. When you carry promises to someone too tired to receive them, don't take it personally. Their silence is exhaustion, not rejection.

The LORD says to Moses: go in and speak to Pharaoh, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land. Guidance: When people will not listen, God may send you to power anyway. The audience changes, but the message stays the same.

The LORD says to Moses: go in and speak to Pharaoh, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land. Guidance: When people will not listen, God may send you to power anyway. The audience changes, but the message stays the same.

Moses: the children of Israel have not listened to me. How shall Pharaoh listen, when I am of uncircumcised lips? Guidance: Discouragement makes us argue against ourselves. The man who carries God's word forgets again that the power was never his words.

The LORD speaks to Moses and to Aaron, and gives them a charge concerning the children of Israel and Pharaoh — to bring the children of Israel out of Egypt. Guidance: When you doubt, God simply re-charges you. He doesn't argue you out of fear; He gives you the next assignment to walk into.

Now the heads of the fathers' houses are listed — Reuben's sons, then Simeon's. The genealogy zooms in on those who will lead the exodus. Guidance: God grounds even great events in actual families. The deliverers are not abstract figures; they have grandfathers.

Now the heads of the fathers' houses are listed — Reuben's sons, then Simeon's. The genealogy zooms in on those who will lead the exodus. Guidance: God grounds even great events in actual families. The deliverers are not abstract figures; they have grandfathers.

Levi's sons: Gershon, Kohath, Merari. Kohath's sons: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, Uzziel. Merari's sons: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites by their generations. Guidance: The future priesthood is being introduced. The tribe that will carry the ark is being named one ancestor at a time.

Levi's sons: Gershon, Kohath, Merari. Kohath's sons: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, Uzziel. Merari's sons: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites by their generations. Guidance: The future priesthood is being introduced. The tribe that will carry the ark is being named one ancestor at a time.

Levi's sons: Gershon, Kohath, Merari. Kohath's sons: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, Uzziel. Merari's sons: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites by their generations. Guidance: The future priesthood is being introduced. The tribe that will carry the ark is being named one ancestor at a time.

Levi's sons: Gershon, Kohath, Merari. Kohath's sons: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, Uzziel. Merari's sons: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites by their generations. Guidance: The future priesthood is being introduced. The tribe that will carry the ark is being named one ancestor at a time.

Amram married Jochebed, his father's sister, and she bore him Aaron and Moses. Amram lived 137 years. Then the line of Izhar and Uzziel is listed. Guidance: Moses' own parentage is on record. The deliverer is a real man with a real mother — the same Jochebed who hid him in the bulrushes.

Amram married Jochebed, his father's sister, and she bore him Aaron and Moses. Amram lived 137 years. Then the line of Izhar and Uzziel is listed. Guidance: Moses' own parentage is on record. The deliverer is a real man with a real mother — the same Jochebed who hid him in the bulrushes.

Amram married Jochebed, his father's sister, and she bore him Aaron and Moses. Amram lived 137 years. Then the line of Izhar and Uzziel is listed. Guidance: Moses' own parentage is on record. The deliverer is a real man with a real mother — the same Jochebed who hid him in the bulrushes.

Aaron marries Elisheba; she bears Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, Ithamar. Eleazar marries a daughter of Putiel, and she bears Phinehas. These are the heads of the Levite fathers' houses. Guidance: A short paragraph plants the names of the entire future priesthood — including the two sons who will die for offering strange fire. The text doesn't whitewash the line.

Aaron marries Elisheba; she bears Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, Ithamar. Eleazar marries a daughter of Putiel, and she bears Phinehas. These are the heads of the Levite fathers' houses. Guidance: A short paragraph plants the names of the entire future priesthood — including the two sons who will die for offering strange fire. The text doesn't whitewash the line.

Aaron marries Elisheba; she bears Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, Ithamar. Eleazar marries a daughter of Putiel, and she bears Phinehas. These are the heads of the Levite fathers' houses. Guidance: A short paragraph plants the names of the entire future priesthood — including the two sons who will die for offering strange fire. The text doesn't whitewash the line.

These are that Aaron and Moses to whom the LORD said: bring out the children of Israel from Egypt according to their armies. These are they who spoke to Pharaoh — to bring out the children of Israel. Guidance: The text pauses to name them again, deliberately. Lest anyone forget who carried this assignment — it was these particular brothers, in this particular family.

These are that Aaron and Moses to whom the LORD said: bring out the children of Israel from Egypt according to their armies. These are they who spoke to Pharaoh — to bring out the children of Israel. Guidance: The text pauses to name them again, deliberately. Lest anyone forget who carried this assignment — it was these particular brothers, in this particular family.

On the day the LORD spoke to Moses in Egypt, He said: I am the LORD. Speak to Pharaoh all I say. Moses answered again: I am of uncircumcised lips. How shall Pharaoh listen to me? Guidance: Moses repeats his old objection. Faith is not a one-time triumph over fear; it is the repeated choice to obey even after the fear comes back.

On the day the LORD spoke to Moses in Egypt, He said: I am the LORD. Speak to Pharaoh all I say. Moses answered again: I am of uncircumcised lips. How shall Pharaoh listen to me? Guidance: Moses repeats his old objection. Faith is not a one-time triumph over fear; it is the repeated choice to obey even after the fear comes back.

Guidance: Moses repeats his old objection. Faith is not a one-time triumph over fear; it is the repeated choice to obey even after the fear comes back.
